content:
How to Become a DevOps Engineer in 2026

Many people ask me — where do I start with DevOps?

Here's the exact structured path.

I built a free platform with everything you need:

🔗 devopsrise.vercel.app

Phase 1 — Linux Fundamentals (2 weeks)

Foundation of everything in DevOps.

• File system, permissions, processes

• Bash scripting basics

• SSH, networking commands

• systemd, cron jobs

Key commands to master:

•
ls -la, chmod, chown, find

•
ps aux, top, kill

•
grep, awk, sed, tail -f

Phase 2 — Git & Version Control (1 week)

• Branching, merging, rebasing

• GitHub workflow, Pull Requests

• Git hooks, conventional commits

• Trunk-based development

Phase 3 — Cloud/AWS (3 weeks)

• EC2, S3, IAM, VPC

• RDS, Lambda, CloudWatch

• Auto Scaling, Load Balancer

• AWS CLI automation

Phase 4 — Docker (2 weeks)

• Dockerfile, docker-compose

• Networking, volumes

• Multi-stage builds

• Security best practices

Phase 5 — Kubernetes (3 weeks)

• Pods, Deployments, Services

• HPA, RBAC, Helm

• Production best practices

• Debugging CrashLoopBackOff

Phase 6 — CI/CD (2 weeks)

• GitHub Actions pipelines

• Jenkins Declarative Pipeline

• Build → Test → Deploy automation

• ArgoCD for GitOps

Phase 7 — Terraform (2 weeks)

• Provision AWS infrastructure as code

• State management, modules

• CI/CD for Terraform

• Security scanning with tfsec

Phase 8 — Monitoring (2 weeks)

• Prometheus + Grafana setup

• AlertManager, Loki for logs

• SLOs and error budgets

• Kubernetes monitoring

Total Timeline

~4 months with 2-3 hours daily practice.

Free Resources

I created a complete FREE platform with:

• ✅ 535+ Real Interview Questions (all 8 topics)

• ✅ 122-Day day-by-day learning plan PDF

• ✅ Commands Cheatsheet (click to copy)

• ✅ Mock Interview Mode with timer

• ✅ Free PDF downloads

🔗 devopsrise.vercel.app

No login. No payment. Just learn.

Tips for Faster Learning

• Build projects after each phase

• Push everything to GitHub

• Write about what you learn (like this!)

• Join DevOps communities on Reddit and Discord

• Apply for jobs after Phase 5-6, not after Phase 8

Drop a comment — which phase are you currently on?

devops #beginners #career #cloud
